calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-20-2022 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-25-2022 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-12-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-25-2022 Off Calendar

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-24-2022 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend The Complaint To Add Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Feb-22-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF AMBER CECCOLA's Motion For Leave To Amend The Complaint To Add Punitive Damages.

Plaintiff's motion to amend is granted. There is a strong policy favoring amendments or the filing of an amended complaint at any stage of the proceeding. (See Honig v. Financial Services of America (1995) 6 Cal.App.4th 960, 965.) Plaintiff's supporting declaration substantially complies with CRC 3.1324(b) and provides good cause of the amendment. (See Gomerman Decl.) Plaintiff recently discovered that defendant purchased a 2-liter boot of beer on the day of the accident and contends that he consumed so much of this "glass of beer" so as to become intoxicated. Based upon this amendment, there is good cause to continue the trial date and re-open discovery. Defendant should have the opportunity to challenge these allegations by conducting further discovery and filing a motion for summary adjudication. The propriety of recovering punitive damages is not always a matter for the trier of fact and may be resolved by summary adjudication. Any request to continue the trial date must be heard in department 206.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/SRB)
